name: ECDLP verified knowledge graph
purpose: A machine-readable, navigable index of the machine-checked (Lean 4 + Mathlib, no sorry or project-defined axioms) structure of the ECDLP landscape for secp256k1: verified ledger rows, their declaration and import dependencies, the corpus claims they verify, the formalization barriers that bound them, and the target-specific route/foundation decisions. Built for a future automated reasoner to load the known-and-verified frontier without re-deriving it.
invariant: Every ledger row resolves to declarations accepted by the Lean kernel with no sorry or project-defined axioms; compiler-trusted native_decide use is disclosed separately. This graph is derived evidence, not a proof.

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_classical_security_profile
theorems.claim: secp256k1 classical attack-resistance profile вҖ” the substrate thesis, one unconditional theorem (`secp256k1_classical_security_profile`: a single kernel-verified conjunction certifying that *every known classically-expressible ECDLP shortcut is blocked* вҖ” `#E = n` (cofactor 1) вҲ§ `Nat.Prime n` (вҮ’ no PohligвҖ“Hellman reduction, no proper subgroup) вҲ§ `t вү  0` (not supersingular) вҲ§ `t вү  1` (not anomalous) вҲ§ `t вүӨ 4p` (Hasse) вҲ§ embedding degree `> 100` (no MOV/FR transfer). Honest scope: this is the *classical structural* envelope, not a hardness proof вҖ” the only proved hardness is the generic `О©(вҲҡn)` bound (`secp256k1_generic_security`), and it is classical-only (Shor breaks ECDLP quantumly). The machine-checked statement of "every classical attack we can express is blocked", not "unbreakable". Assembles proved conjuncts; inherits `Lean.ofReduceBool` from them)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_twist_security_profile`, `secp256k1_twist_order_factorization`, `secp256k1_twist_maxprime_prime
theorems.claim: secp256k1 quadratic-twist security certificate вҖ” a limitation, not a strength (`secp256k1_twist_security_profile`: the twist order `#бәј = 2p+2вҲ’n` factors exactly as `3В·13В·3319В·22639В·Q` with `Q` a 220-bit prime (`secp256k1_twist_maxprime_prime`, full Pratt certificate; `secp256k1_twist_order_factorization`). Two honest consequences: (1) the cofactor `3В·13В·3319В·22639 = 114286177161 вүҲ 2ВівҒ· > 1` is nontrivial вҖ” unlike the simple point group of `E`, the twist has genuine small-order subgroups a point can be confined to; (2) `2вҒ№ < Q < 2вҒ°`, so generic twist-DLP costs `вүҲ вҲҡQ < 2вҒ°`, below the curve's `вүҲ2вҒё`. This is the machine-checked reason single-coordinate (`x`-only / Montgomery-ladder) secp256k1 code must validate points. Inherits `Lean.ofReduceBool` from `native_decide` + Pratt)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_frobenius_norm`, `secp256k1_frobenius_trace`, `secp256k1_four_p_eq_trace_sq
theorems.claim: secp256k1 CM Frobenius certificate (`N(ПҖ)=aвҲ’ab+b=p` `secp256k1_frobenius_norm`, `Tr(ПҖ)=2aвҲ’b=p+1вҲ’n` `secp256k1_frobenius_trace`, `4p=t+3b` `secp256k1_four_p_eq_trace_sq` вҖ” the integer CM data for `ПҖ=a+bПү`; with `t<4p` this pins `End(E)вҠ—в„ҡ=в„ҡ(вҲҡвҲ’3)`, the order `в„Ө[Пү]` of discriminant вҲ’3 (corrects mistaken CM composites). theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_ОЁвӮғ_eval_glv`, `secp256k1_preОЁвӮ…_eval_glv_invariant`, `secp256k1_preОЁвӮҮ_eval_glv_invariant`, `secp256k1_ОЁвӮӮSq_eval_glv_invariant
theorems.claim: GLV covariance law of the division polynomials (`ПҲ_m(ОІx) = ОІ^((mвҲ’1)/2)В·ПҲ_m(x)`: for `j=0` each `ПҲ_m` sits on one exponent-residue mod 3, so `ОІ` acts by a scalar вҖ” `ОЁвӮғ(ОІx)=ОІВ·ОЁвӮғ(x)` (`secp256k1_ОЁвӮғ_eval_glv`), and `preОЁ' 5`, `preОЁ' 7`, `ОЁвӮӮSq` are `ОІ`-invariant (`secp256k1_{preОЁвӮ…,preОЁвӮҮ,ОЁвӮӮSq}_eval_glv_invariant`), from the explicit forms + `ОІВі=1`. Explains the failed experiment #100: `Res(preОЁ' 5(X), preОЁ' 5(ОІX)) = Res(preОЁ' 5, preОЁ' 5) = 0` identically because `preОЁ' 5` is `ОІ`-invariant вҖ” the split/inert resultant test was ill-posed, not a signal. The residual is closed by a kernel-only `linear_combination`, but the underlying cube fact `ОІВі=1` lifts `Secp256k1.beta_field_eigenvalue` (`native_decide`), so these results inherit `Lean.ofReduceBool` вҖ” compiler-trusted, not kernel-only)

Compiler-trusted: the orbit facts lift `Secp256k1.beta_field_eigenvalue` (`native_decide`), so they inherit `Lean.ofReduceBool`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.cube_covariant_support`, `exists_comp_X_pow_of_invariant`, `exists_X_mul_comp_of_covariant`, `secp256k1_ОЁвӮғ_compressed`, `secp256k1_preОЁвӮ…_compressed`, `secp256k1_preОЁвӮҮ_compressed`, `secp256k1_preОЁвӮү_natDegree
theorems.claim: Compressed division polynomials вҖ” cube-covariance structural core + `mвҲҲ{3,5,7}` forms (`cube_covariant_support`/`exists_comp_X_pow_of_invariant`/`exists_X_mul_comp_of_covariant`: over any field with a primitive cube root `ОІ`, `p(ОІX)=cВ·p(X)` forces every support exponent `k` to satisfy `ОІ^k=c`, hence `c=1 вҮ’ pвҲҲF[XВі]` and `c=ОІ вҮ’ pвҲҲXВ·F[XВі]` with the matching `deg` drop вҖ” the foundation-free structural heart. Applied unconditionally to secp256k1's concrete forms: `ОЁвӮғ=XВ·RвӮғ(XВі)` (`deg RвӮғ=1`), `preОЁвӮ…=RвӮ…(XВі)` (`deg RвӮ…=4`), `preОЁвӮҮ=RвӮҮ(XВі)` (`deg RвӮҮ=8`), plus the new bare degree `deg preОЁвӮү=40`. For `mвҲҲ{9,11,13}` the compressed form is stated conditional on the polynomial-level `ОІ`-covariance of `ПҲ_m` (`preОЁ'_compressed_of_{invariant,covariant}`) вҖ” the isolated missing lemma (repo has only the `eval`-level covariance, `mвҲҲ{3,5,7}`). Concrete forms inherit `Lean.ofReduceBool` from `native_decide` degree facts; the structural core does not. Drafted with the Kimi K3 proof-drafter, kernel-verified in CI)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_glvHom_eq_zsmul
theorems.claim: GLV eigenvalue property `ПҶ=[k]`, conditional on cyclicity (if `E(рқ”Ҫ_p)` is cyclic then `glvHom` is multiplication by a fixed `k:в„Ө` with `(k+k+1)вҖўP=0` for all `P` вҖ” the geometric `ОІ`-action *is* scalar `[О»]`; a genuine reduction isolating the one deep missing input, point-counting `#E=n`, as the explicit `[IsAddCyclic]` hypothesis)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_glv_preserves_dlog
theorems.claim: GLV/CM endomorphism preserves the discrete log (no-go) (`secp256k1_glv_preserves_dlog`: for every scalar `m:в„Ө` and points with `Q = mвҖўP`, `glvHom Q = mвҖўglvHom P` вҖ” mapping a DLP instance `(P,Q)` through the GLV endomorphism gives another instance with the same secret `m`. The endomorphism is a DLP self-reduction that is the identity on the exponent, so *this reduction* cannot shrink it: the formal core of the believed "CM-by-`в„Ө[О¶вӮғ]` gives no asymptotic advantage against ECDLP" вҖ” a scoped, not universal, no-go (it does not rule out some unknown faster algorithm using `в„Ө[О¶вӮғ]`; it proves *this* map is exponent-preserving). The order-3 automorphism buys only a constant factor (~вҲҡ3 rho, ~2Г— scalar-mult). Unconditional as an identity on the exponent)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_glv_single_scalar
theorems.claim: GLV structure is one scalar on base and target (no-go, cond. cyclicity) (`secp256k1_glv_single_scalar`: under cyclicity, on a DLP instance `Q = mвҖўP` the endomorphism satisfies `glvHom P = kвҖўP` and `glvHom Q = kвҖўQ` for the one eigenvalue `k` with `k+k+1вүЎ0` вҖ” it multiplies base and target by the *same* `k`, leaving `m` invariant. The entire CM "extra structure" is this single known relation; no new unknown, no new equation on `m`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.P256.P256_О”_ne_zero` (+ `instance : P256.IsElliptic`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.P256.P256_generator_equation

Makes concrete that secp256k1's `j=0`/CM structure is a *special* feature, not generic)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.P256.Primality.p256_p_prime` (+ `instance : Fact (Nat.Prime Ecdlp.P256.p)`)
theorems.claim: NIST P-256 field prime `p` is prime (Pratt certificate) (`p256_p_prime`: full recursive Pratt / `lucas_primality` certificate for `p = 2вҒөвҒ¶вҲ’2вҒҙ+2вҒ№+2вҒ№вҒ¶вҲ’1`, 9 recursive nodes, witness `a=6`. Discharges the `[Fact p.Prime]` hypothesis, so P-256's `IsElliptic` instance and Mathlib group law become unconditional вҖ” exactly as for secp256k1. The second curve's primality via the *same* generator `scripts/pratt_certificate.py`, the honest cross-domain proof that the machinery is curve-agnostic)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.P256.Primality.p256_n_prime` (+ `instance : Fact (Nat.Prime Ecdlp.P256.n)`)

Both P-256 primes (`p`, `n`) now proved by the same curve-agnostic generator)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.P256.p256_embedding_degree_gt_100
theorems.claim: NIST P-256 has no small embedding degree (MOV/FreyвҖ“RГјck resistance) (`p256_embedding_degree_gt_100`: for every `1 вүӨ k вүӨ 100`, `p^k вүў 1 (mod n)`, so P-256's embedding degree exceeds 100 вҖ” the MOV/FR pairing transfer would need an intractable `рқ”Ҫ_{p^k}`, `k>100`. The second live domain's first attack-boundary rung, mirroring secp256k1's `EmbeddingDegree.lean`; pure modular arithmetic on the verified primes `p`, `n`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.P256.p256_trace_ordinary_nonanomalous
theorems.claim: NIST P-256 is ordinary, non-anomalous, Hasse-consistent (Smart/SSSA boundary) (`p256_trace_ordinary_nonanomalous`: with `#E=n`, the trace `t=p+1вҲ’n` satisfies `tвү 0` (not supersingular), `tвү 1` (not anomalous вҖ” Smart/SSSA inapplicable), `tвүӨ4p` (Hasse; `t` is 127-bit, cofactor 1). The second live domain's SSSA boundary rung, mirroring secp256k1's `TraceOfFrobenius.lean`; conditional on the published `#E=n` as for secp256k1)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.P256.p256_generator_addOrderOf` (+ `p256_generator_nonsingular`, `p256_generator_nsmul_n_eq_zero`)

Proof: `nвҖўG=0` (native double-and-add over рқ”Ҫ_p) + `Gвү 0`, `n` prime вҮ’ `addOrderOf_eq_prime`. Mirrors secp256k1's `GeneratorOrder.lean`; server-verified on the warm Lean toolchain then CI. Does NOT give the strong keystone `#E=n`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.P256.instFiniteP256Point

theorems.name: Ecdlp.P256.p256_n_dvd_card_point` (+ `p256_grp_card`)
theorems.claim: n вҲЈ #E(рқ”Ҫ_p)` for P-256 вҖ” base-point order divides the curve cardinality (`p256_n_dvd_card_point`: `P256.n вҲЈ Nat.card P256.toAffine.Point`, by Lagrange on `вҹЁGвҹ©` (order the proved `n`) + finiteness. The first verified fact about P-256's `#E` itself вҖ” all prior P-256 point-counting stayed inside `вҹЁGвҹ©`. Weak keystone: pins `#E вҲҲ {n, 2n, 3n, вҖҰ}`. The strong keystone `#E = n` is NOT proved for P-256: the secp256k1 `j=0` / `вҲ’7`-non-cube 2-torsion route is `j=0`-specific and does not transfer (P-256 has `cвӮ„=144вү 0`, `jвү 0`), and the general Hasse bound is a Mathlib gap. Pure-kernel)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.P256.p256_twist_security_profile`, `p256_twist_order_factorization`, `p256_twist_maxprime_prime
theorems.claim: NIST P-256 quadratic-twist security certificate вҖ” cross-curve companion of the secp256k1 twist limitation (`p256_twist_security_profile`: the number `2p+2вҲ’n` factors exactly as `3В·5В·13В·179В·Q` with `Q` a 241-bit prime (`p256_twist_maxprime_prime`, full Pratt certificate; `p256_twist_order_factorization`). theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_four_torsion_ncard_le
theorems.claim: Tight `#E[4](рқ”Ҫ_p) вүӨ 16` point-cardinality bound (`secp256k1_four_torsion_ncard_le`: `Set.ncard {P \| 4вҖўP=0} вүӨ 16 = 4` вҖ” the even-index entry of the `#E[n]` count family, at the tight value. `n=4` mixes self-paired 2-torsion (`y=0`) with `Вұy`-paired primitive 4-torsion, so instead of the generic `вүӨ2m+1` we split `E[4] = E[2] вҠ” PвӮ„` (`PвӮ„ = {4вҖўP=0, 2вҖўPвү 0}`): `#E[2] вүӨ 4` (`secp256k1_two_torsion_ncard_le`) + a `вүӨ2`-to-`1` fiber count `#PвӮ„ вүӨ 12` over the six `preОЁвӮ„`-roots вҖ” via the 4-torsion bridge `4вҖўP=0 вҹә ПҲвӮ„=0` and `ПҲвӮ„=4yВ·(xвҒ¶+140xВівҲ’392)`, a primitive point (`2вҖўPвү 0`вҹ№`yвү 0`) forces `preОЁвӮ„(x)=0`. `4+12=16`. Finiteness from the global `Finite W.Point` instance; reuses the `px`/`py` curve-fiber machinery of `TorsionPointCount`. The even-index tight companion of `#E[3]вүӨ9`/`#E[5]вүӨ25`/`#E[7]вүӨ49`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_torsionBy_inf_eq_gcd
theorems.claim: Torsion intersection = gcd (`secp256k1_torsionBy_inf_eq_gcd`: `E[m] вҠ“ E[n] = E[gcd m n]` вҖ” the full lattice identity generalizing the coprime-disjoint leaf; via the `addOrderOf вҲЈ В·` characterization of `torsionBy`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_glvPoint_bijOn_torsion
theorems.claim: GLV/CM automorphism permutes the `n`-torsion (`secp256k1_glvPoint_bijOn_torsion`: the order-3 GLV endomorphism `glvPoint` restricts to a `Set.BijOn` of `E[n] = {P \| nвҖўP=0}` onto itself вҖ” additivity sends `E[n]` into itself, `glvPointВі = id` gives surjectivity; a new endomorphismвҶ”torsion interaction, no point-counting/Weil needed)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_glvPoint_fixed_coprime_three
theorems.claim: GLV fixed locus meets `E[n]` trivially when `gcd(n,3)=1` (`secp256k1_glvPoint_fixed_coprime_three`: `Coprime n 3 вҶ’ nвҖўP=0 вҶ’ glvPoint P = P вҶ’ P = 0` вҖ” the coprime-3 fixed-point-freeness of the CM automorphism, the group-law shadow of `ker(ПҶвҲ’1) вҠҶ E[3]`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_scalar_no_proper_subgroup
theorems.claim: secp256k1 scalar group `в„Ө/n` has no proper nontrivial subgroup (`secp256k1_scalar_no_proper_subgroup`: every `H : AddSubgroup (ZMod n)` is `вҠҘ` or `вҠӨ` вҖ” the full subgroup-lattice dichotomy from `n` prime, strictly stronger than the element-level generator fact; small-subgroup / invalid-subgroup confinement-attack resistance)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_scalar_num_generators
theorems.claim: secp256k1 has exactly `nвҲ’1` full-order private keys (`secp256k1_scalar_num_generators`: `#{a : ZMod n \| addOrderOf a = n} = nвҲ’1` вҖ” every nonzero scalar generates, `ПҶ(n)=nвҲ’1`; the quantitative generator/valid-key count)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Schnorr.ecdsa_nonce_reuse_recovers
theorems.claim: ECDSA nonce-reuse private-key recovery (`ecdsa_nonce_reuse_recovers`: two signatures `sбөўВ·k = zбөў + rВ·x` sharing nonce `k`, `sвӮҒвү sвӮӮ`, `rвү 0` вҮ’ `k=(zвӮҒвҲ’zвӮӮ)/(sвӮҒвҲ’sвӮӮ) вҲ§ x=(sвӮҒkвҲ’zвӮҒ)/r` вҖ” the exact algebra behind the Sony PS3 / Android-Bitcoin key thefts; stated over any field, no adversary/probability model)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Schnorr.ecdsa_known_nonce_recovers_key
theorems.claim: ECDSA known/leaked-nonce private-key recovery (`ecdsa_known_nonce_recovers_key`: `sВ·k=z+rВ·x` with known `k`, `rвү 0` вҮ’ `x=(skвҲ’z)/r` вҖ” the biased/leaked-nonce (HNP) scenario, distinct from nonce-reuse)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_pointX_neg`, `secp256k1_pointX_neg_zsmul_add`, `Ecdlp.Schnorr.ecdsa_sibling_signing_equation`, `ecdsa_sibling_scalars
theorems.claim: ECDSA signature malleability вҖ” the algebraic core of BIP-62 rule 5 / BIP-146 low-`s` (every signature `(r,s)` has a sibling `(r,вҲ’s)` verifying the same message/key: `ecdsa_sibling_signing_equation` вҖ” the signing identity holds for `(вҲ’s,вҲ’k)`; `ecdsa_sibling_scalars` вҖ” `u/(вҲ’s) = вҲ’(u/s)` negates both verifier scalars; `secp256k1_pointX_neg` вҖ” point negation fixes the `x`-coordinate (`вҲ’(x,y)=(x,вҲ’y)`, `aвӮҒ=aвӮғ=0`); `secp256k1_pointX_neg_zsmul_add` вҖ” the negated verification point has the same `pointX`. Honest scope: identity-level algebra, no `Verify` predicate, no hash/DER level, no `ZMod p вҶ’ ZMod n` reduction of `x`. Field lemmas pure-kernel; curve lemma under `[Fact p.Prime]` (discharged via Pratt вҮ’ compiler-trusted transitively))

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Schnorr.shamir_reconstruct
theorems.claim: Shamir secret-sharing reconstruction (`shamir_reconstruct`: for a degree-`<t` sharing polynomial `f` and `t = #s` distinct nodes, `f.eval 0` (the secret) `= (Lagrange.interpolate s v (f.eval вҲҳ v)).eval 0` вҖ” the information-theoretic recovery direction of Shamir's `(t,n)` SSS; secret = `f(0)`, shares = `(vбөў, f(vбөў))`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.MovReduction.mov_random_q_success_probability
theorems.claim: Number of generators of a cyclic group = `ПҶ(n)` (`mov_random_q_success_probability`: in a cyclic group of order `n`, `#{g \| orderOf g = n} = ПҶ(n)` вҖ” the count of full-order elements; underpins the MOV/FreyвҖ“RГјck random-`Q` success probability `ПҶ(n)/n`. Layer-3 generated, closed the last open Layer-3 `Targets/` stem of its batch (the intentional smoke stem stays open), corpus claim `mov-random-q-success-probability-006`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.SвӮғ_eq_zero_of_chord`, `secp256k1_semaev_three_chord
theorems.claim: Semaev's 3rd summation polynomial вҖ” forward direction (`SвӮғ_eq_zero_of_chord`: for `y = xВі + aВ·x + b` over a field, if `(xвӮҒ,yвӮҒ), (xвӮӮ,yвӮӮ)` are curve points with `xвӮҒ вү  xвӮӮ` and `xвӮғ` is the `x`-coordinate of their chord-sum, then `SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,xвӮғ) = 0` вҖ” equivalently `PвӮҒ+PвӮӮ+PвӮғ = O вҮ’ SвӮғ = 0`; `secp256k1_semaev_three_chord` specializes it to secp256k1 `y = xВі + 7`. First Semaev summation polynomial formalized in Lean/Mathlib (a green-field `BARRIERS.md` item). Certificate designed + verified by exact symbolic elimination (sympy: GrГ¶bner + iterated resultant), kernel-checked via `linear_combination`. A construction, not an attack вҖ” a necessary collinearity condition that by itself computes nothing about any discrete log)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.SвӮғ_eq_zero_of_tangent`, `secp256k1_semaev_three_tangent
theorems.claim: Semaev's `SвӮғ` вҖ” tangent / doubling case (`SвӮғ_eq_zero_of_tangent`: the `xвӮҒ = xвӮӮ` companion of the chord case вҖ” if `(xвӮҒ,yвӮҒ)` is a curve point and `xвӮғ` is the `x`-coordinate of `2В·(xвӮҒ,yвӮҒ)` (cleared doubling form `4В·yвӮҒВ·(xвӮғ+2В·xвӮҒ) = (3В·xвӮҒ+a)`), then `SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮҒ,xвӮғ) = 0`; `secp256k1_semaev_three_tangent` specializes to secp256k1. With the chord case this completes the `SвӮғ` forward direction in every nondegenerate case. No `yвӮҒВ·yвӮӮ` cross term arises, so a single certified `linear_combination` suffices)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.secp256k1_semaev_three_point
theorems.claim: Semaev's `SвӮғ` on actual secp256k1 curve points (`secp256k1_semaev_three_point`: for genuine points of the Mathlib elliptic curve `secp256k1.toAffine.Point`, the group relation `PвӮҒ+PвӮӮ+PвӮғ = O` with `x(PвӮҒ) вү  x(PвӮӮ)` implies `SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,xвӮғ) = 0`. Connects the abstract chord identity to Mathlib's formalized elliptic-curve group law by unfolding `Point.add_of_X_ne` / `slope` / `addX` to recover `xвӮғ = x(PвӮҒ+PвӮӮ)` вҖ” the honest statement whose hypothesis is the actual curve addition, not a raw coordinate equation)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.secp256k1_semaev_three_point_double
theorems.claim: Semaev's `SвӮғ` on actual secp256k1 points вҖ” tangent case (`secp256k1_semaev_three_point_double`: the `PвӮҒ = PвӮӮ` companion вҖ” for genuine points of `secp256k1.toAffine.Point`, `2вҖўPвӮҒ + PвӮғ = O` with `PвӮҒ` not 2-torsion (`yвӮҒ вү  negY xвӮҒ yвӮҒ`) implies `SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮҒ,xвӮғ) = 0`, via Mathlib's tangent doubling `Point.add_of_Y_ne`. With the chord point theorem, the `SвӮғ` forward direction now holds on Mathlib's actual elliptic-curve group law in every nondegenerate case)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.secp256k1_point_decomposition_semaev
theorems.claim: Point decomposition вҮ’ Semaev relation (index-calculus entry point) (`secp256k1_point_decomposition_semaev`: if a curve point `R` decomposes as a sum `R = PвӮҒ + PвӮӮ` with `x(PвӮҒ) вү  x(PвӮӮ)`, then `SвӮғ(x(PвӮҒ), x(PвӮӮ), x(R)) = 0` вҖ” every 2-term decomposition of `R` lies on the Semaev variety. This is the structural reduction index calculus rests on: the search for decompositions is confined to the roots of a single codimension-1 polynomial condition, not the whole group. Proof recasts `R = PвӮҒ+PвӮӮ` as `PвӮҒ+PвӮӮ+(вҲ’R)=0` and uses `x(вҲ’R) = x(R)` (`Point.neg_some`) to hand off to `secp256k1_semaev_three_point`. Forward/structural half only вҖ” records *where* solutions live, not the cost of *finding* them; over `рқ”Ҫ_p` that root-finding is the studied `Оҳ(вҲҡn)` barrier)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.secp256k1_point_decomposition_semaev_double
theorems.claim: Point decomposition вҮ’ Semaev relation вҖ” doubling case (`secp256k1_point_decomposition_semaev_double`: the `PвӮҒ = PвӮӮ` companion вҖ” if `R = 2В·PвӮҒ` with `PвӮҒ` not 2-torsion, then `SвӮғ(x(PвӮҒ), x(PвӮҒ), x(R)) = 0`. With the generic-sum theorem, covers every 2-term decomposition of `R`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.secp256k1_SвӮғpoly_natDegree
theorems.claim: Semaev polynomial has degree exactly 2 in each variable (`secp256k1_SвӮғpoly_natDegree`: for `xвӮҒ вү  xвӮӮ` over `рқ”Ҫ_p`, `SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,В·)` is a degree-exactly-2 univariate polynomial, leading coefficient `(xвӮҒвҲ’xвӮӮ) вү  0`. Base case of the classical `deg_{xбөў} SвӮҳ = 2^{mвҲ’2}` degree tower вҖ” the doubling that makes higher-order decompositions blow up. Via `Polynomial.natDegree_quadratic`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.secp256k1_decomposition_completions_le_two
theorems.claim: Bounded decomposition fan-out (index-calculus cost ingredient) (`secp256k1_decomposition_completions_le_two`: fixing a base coordinate `xвӮҒ` and the target `x_R` with `xвӮҒ вү  x_R`, at most 2 field elements `xвӮӮ` complete a 2-decomposition `SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,x_R)=0` вҖ” any finite set of completions has card `вүӨ 2`. A degree-2 polynomial over a field has `вүӨ 2` roots, so the index-calculus relation graph has bounded fan-out at each factor-base point. The one exact, kernel-checked quantitative fact under the prime-field cost analysis вҖ” *not* the hardness claim itself, which stays an open conjecture; see `BARRIERS.md`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.SвӮғ_root_of_eq_zero
theorems.claim: Semaev's `SвӮғ` вҖ” reverse direction (the two roots) (`SвӮғ_root_of_eq_zero`: `SвӮғ` is quadratic in `xвӮғ` with leading coefficient `(xвӮҒвҲ’xвӮӮ)`; if `(xвӮҒ,yвӮҒ), (xвӮӮ,yвӮӮ)` lie on `y = xВі + aВ·x + b` and `SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,xвӮғ) = 0`, then `(xвӮҒвҲ’xвӮӮ)В·xвӮғ` equals `(yвӮӮвҲ’yвӮҒ) вҲ’ (xвӮҒ+xвӮӮ)(xвӮҒвҲ’xвӮӮ)` or `(yвӮӮ+yвӮҒ) вҲ’ (xвӮҒ+xвӮӮ)(xвӮҒвҲ’xвӮӮ)` вҖ” the cleared `x`-coordinates of `PвӮҒ+PвӮӮ` and `PвӮҒвҲ’PвӮӮ`. Via a sympy-certified master factorization `(xвӮҒвҲ’xвӮӮ)В·SвӮғ = (DВ·xвӮғвҲ’RвӮҠ)(DВ·xвӮғвҲ’RвӮӢ)`, a pure `ring` identity, then `mul_eq_zero`; needs no `xвӮҒ вү  xвӮӮ`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.SвӮғ_eq_zero_iff`, `secp256k1_semaev_three_iff
theorems.claim: Semaev's `SвӮғ` вҖ” full characterization (iff) (`SвӮғ_eq_zero_iff`: for on-curve `(xвӮҒ,yвӮҒ), (xвӮӮ,yвӮӮ)` with `xвӮҒ вү  xвӮӮ`, `SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,xвӮғ) = 0 вҹә (xвӮҒвҲ’xвӮӮ)(xвӮҒ+xвӮӮ+xвӮғ) = (yвӮӮвҲ’yвӮҒ)  вҲЁ  = (yвӮӮ+yвӮҒ)` вҖ” i.e. `xвӮғ` is the `x`-coordinate of `PвӮҒ+PвӮӮ` or of `PвӮҒвҲ’PвӮӮ`; `secp256k1_semaev_three_iff` specializes to secp256k1. Combines forward + reverse: the complete statement of `SвӮғ` as the elimination of `yвӮҒ,yвӮӮ` from "three points sum to `O`")

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.SвӮ„`, `SвӮ„_eq_zero_of_common_root`, `secp256k1_semaev_four_of_common_root
theorems.claim: Semaev's 4th summation polynomial `SвӮ„` вҖ” definition + forward direction (`SвӮ„ = Res_X(SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,X), SвӮғ(xвӮғ,xвӮ„,X))` via Mathlib's `Polynomial.resultant _ _ 2 2`; `SвӮ„_eq_zero_of_common_root`: a shared root `XвӮҖ` of the two `SвӮғ` slices вҖ” e.g. `XвӮҖ = x(PвӮҒ+PвӮӮ) = x(PвӮғ+PвӮ„)` вҖ” forces `SвӮ„ = 0`, over any commutative ring, via the resultant BГ©zout identity `exists_mul_add_mul_eq_C_resultant`. `secp256k1_semaev_four_of_common_root` specializes to secp256k1. The first rung above `SвӮғ` вҖ” the recursion index calculus over `рқ”Ҫ_{p^k}` uses. First `SвӮ„` in Lean)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.resultant_eq_zero_of_common_root
theorems.claim: Semaev forward direction generalized to all `n` вҖ” the resultant recursion engine (`resultant_eq_zero_of_common_root`: for *any* two univariate polynomials `f, g` over a commutative ring sharing an evaluation-root `XвӮҖ` (`f(XвӮҖ) = g(XвӮҖ) = 0`), `Res(f,g,m,n) = 0`, provided the Sylvester sizes are not both zero. Every Semaev step `SвӮҷвӮҠвӮҒ = Res_X(SвӮҷ(вҖҰ,X), SвӮғ(вҖҰ,X))` inherits its forward direction from this one lemma at level `n`; `SвӮ„_eq_zero_of_common_root` is now literally the instance `f = SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,В·)`, `g = SвӮғ(xвӮғ,xвӮ„,В·)`, and `SвӮ…, SвӮҶ, вҖҰ` are discharged verbatim with `f` the previous slice. The mechanism-level generalization of the Semaev forward direction to the whole infinite `SвӮҷ` family вҖ” via the BГ©zout identity `exists_mul_add_mul_eq_C_resultant`, over any commutative ring, no field or closure)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.SвӮғpoly_eval`, `SвӮ„_block_swap`, `SвӮ„_symmвӮҒвӮӮ`, `SвӮ„_symmвӮғвӮ„
theorems.claim: *(alternate/supporting)* `SвӮ„` infrastructure вҖ” the `SвӮғ`-as-polynomial bridge `SвӮғpoly_eval` (`(SвӮғpoly вҖҰ).eval xвӮғ = SвӮғ вҖҰ`) and `SвӮ„`'s full symmetry: block swap `SвӮ„_block_swap` `(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ)вҶ”(xвӮғ,xвӮ„)` (from `resultant_comm`) and within-pair `SвӮ„_symmвӮҒвӮӮ`, `SвӮ„_symmвӮғвӮ„` вҖ” so `SвӮ„` is symmetric on all of `{xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,xвӮғ,xвӮ„}`, the symmetry a summation polynomial must have

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.SвӮ„_common_root_of_eq_zero`, `secp256k1_semaev_four_common_root_of_eq_zero
theorems.claim: Semaev's `SвӮ„` вҖ” reverse/meaning direction (`SвӮ„_common_root_of_eq_zero`: over a field, for on-curve `(xвӮҒ,yвӮҒ), (xвӮӮ,yвӮӮ)` with `xвӮҒ вү  xвӮӮ` and `SвӮ„(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,xвӮғ,xвӮ„) = 0`, the two `SвӮғ` slices share a root `XвӮҖ` in the field вҖ” the converse of `SвӮ„_eq_zero_of_common_root`. Since `SвӮ„` is the resultant, its vanishing forces a shared root; it lies *in* `K` because `SвӮғ(xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ,В·)` splits with the explicitly known roots `x(PвӮҒВұPвӮӮ)`. Via `resultant_eq_prod_eval`; `secp256k1_semaev_four_common_root_of_eq_zero` specializes. With the forward direction, `SвӮ„` now holds both ways)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.SвӮғpoly_master_factor
theorems.claim: SвӮғ`-slice two-root factorization over `F[X]` (`SвӮғpoly_master_factor`: `(xвӮҒвҲ’xвӮӮ)В·SвӮғpoly = (DВ·X вҲ’ RвӮҠ)В·(DВ·X вҲ’ RвӮӢ)` as a polynomial identity in `F[X]` вҖ” the lift of the scalar master factorization, exhibiting `SвӮғ`'s degree-2 slice as split with the explicit roots `x(PвӮҒВұPвӮӮ)`; the key input to the `SвӮ„` reverse)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Semaev.SвӮғ_symmвӮҒвӮӮ`, `SвӮғ_symmвӮӮвӮғ
theorems.claim: *(alternate/supporting)* Semaev `SвӮғ` full symmetry вҖ” `SвӮғ_symmвӮҒвӮӮ` (swap `xвӮҒ,xвӮӮ`) and `SвӮғ_symmвӮӮвӮғ` (swap `xвӮӮ,xвӮғ`), so `SвӮғ` is symmetric in all three `x`-arguments, as a summation polynomial must be

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_double_x_eq_ОҰвӮӮ_div_ОЁвӮӮSq`, `secp256k1_ОҰвӮӮ
theorems.claim: Multiplication-by-2 `x`-coordinate formula in Mathlib's canonical division polynomials (`secp256k1_double_x_eq_ОҰвӮӮ_div_ОЁвӮӮSq`: for a non-2-torsion point `P=(x,y)` on `y=xВі+7`, the doubled `x`-coordinate `addX x x (slope x x y y) = (ОҰ 2)(x)/(ОЁвӮӮSq)(x)` вҖ” with Mathlib's own `ОҰ`/`ОЁSq` division polynomials, `ОҰ 2 = XвҒҙвҲ’56X` (`secp256k1_ОҰвӮӮ`), `ОЁвӮӮSq = 4XВі+28 = 4y`. This is the base case `n=2` of the general multiplication formula `x([n]P)=ОҰвӮҷ/ОЁSqвӮҷ` вҖ” the engine of the general `ПҲвӮҷ`-vanishing вҹә `n`-torsion bridge (`notes/FOUNDATIONS.md` rung 4). Unlike the per-`n` bridges (which used ad-hoc reduced polynomials like `3xвҒҙ+84x`), this states the *canonical* `ОҰвӮҷ/ОЁSqвӮҷ` shape. The general-`n` formula, by induction on the division-polynomial recurrence, is the missing Mathlib rung вҖ” absent from v4.31, present only in a stalled upstream PR)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_double_y_eq_ПүвӮӮ
theorems.claim: Multiplication-by-2 `y`-coordinate formula `y(2вҖўP) = ПүвӮӮ/(2y)Ві` (N7-uniform S3a base) (`secp256k1_double_y_eq_ПүвӮӮ`: for a non-2-torsion point `P=(x,y)` on `y=xВі+7`, the doubled `y`-coordinate `addY x x y (slope x x y y) = (xвҒ¶ + 140xВі вҲ’ 392)/(2y)Ві` вҖ” the `y`-analogue of the `n=2` `x`-formula and the first `y`-coordinate division-polynomial value in the repo, i.e. `y(2вҖўP) = ПүвӮӮ/ПҲвӮӮВі` with `ПҲвӮӮ = 2y` and the `n=2` `y`-coordinate ("omega") polynomial `ПүвӮӮ = xвҒ¶ + 140xВі вҲ’ 392`. Mathlib has no `Пү` division polynomial (open `TODO`); the value is derived from the group law's doubling `addY` and closed by one certified `linear_combination` against the curve equation and the slope relation `2yВ·в„“ = 3x`. Base case of node S3a of the N7-uniform build (`BARRIERS.md В§B3`; target `n7_uniform_secp256k1_x`). Honest scope: fixed `n=2` coordinate identity only; the uniform `y([n]P)=ПүвӮҷ/ПҲвӮҷВі` and the general bivariate `ПүвӮҷ` remain open. No `native_decide`, no new axioms)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_two_nsmul_coords
theorems.claim: Point-level doubling `2вҖўP = (ОҰвӮӮ/ОЁвӮӮSq, ПүвӮӮ/(2y)Ві)` on secp256k1's group (N7-uniform S3a) (`secp256k1_two_nsmul_coords`: for a nonsingular non-2-torsion `P=(x,y)` on `y=xВі+7`, if the double `2 вҖў Point.some x y h` is the affine point `Point.some X Y h'` then both coordinates are pinned to their division-polynomial values: `X = ОҰвӮӮ(x)/ОЁвӮӮSq(x)` and `Y = (xвҒ¶+140xВівҲ’392)/(2y)Ві`. Connects the two landed coordinate identities to the actual group operation `2 вҖў P` on `secp256k1.toAffine.Point` (via `two_nsmul` + `Point.add_self_of_Y_ne` + the doubled point's coordinate injectivity) вҖ” the `n=2` Point-level instance of the open target `n7_uniform_secp256k1_x` and the base case of node S3a of the N7-uniform build. Honest scope: fixed `n=2` only; the uniform statement for all `n` remains open. No `native_decide`, no new axioms)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_triple_y_eq_ПүвӮғ
theorems.claim: Multiplication-by-3 `y`-coordinate formula `y(3вҖўP) = ПүвӮғ/ПҲвӮғВі` (N7-uniform S3a) (`secp256k1_triple_y_eq_ПүвӮғ`: in the chord-tangent slope parametrisation `sвӮӮ` (`2yВ·sвӮӮ=3x`), `sвӮғ` (`(sвӮӮвҲ’3x)В·sвӮғ = вҲ’(sвӮӮ(sвӮӮвҲ’3x)+y)вҲ’y`) of the `x`-triple, the tripled `y`-coordinate `sвӮғВ·(sвӮӮвҲ’sвӮғ)вҲ’y` equals `ПүвӮғ/(3xвҒҙ+84x)Ві` with the `n=3` `y`-coordinate ("omega") polynomial `ПүвӮғ = yВ·(x+1540xвҒ№вҲ’87024xвҒ¶вҲ’109760xВівҲ’1229312)` вҖ” bivariate, the `y` factor being the odd-`n` shape. Companion to the `x`-triple `x(3вҖўP)=ОҰвӮғ/ОЁSqвӮғ` and extends the `n=2` `y`-formula. Mathlib has no `Пү` division polynomial (open `TODO`). Proof mirrors the `x`-triple: Step A clears `sвӮғ` via `hв„“3` into an `sвӮғ`-free `W`-expression, Step B is the `sвӮғ`-free master identity cleared by `(2y)Ві` (CAS-derived `linear_combination` cofactors), then cancel `(sвӮӮвҲ’3x)Ві`. Honest scope: fixed `n=3` coordinate identity; the uniform `y([n]P)=ПүвӮҷ/ПҲвӮҷВі` remains open. No `native_decide`, no new axioms)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_ОҰвӮғ`, `secp256k1_ОЁSqвӮғ`, `secp256k1_ОҰвӮғ_eval`, `secp256k1_ОЁSqвӮғ_eval
theorems.claim: n = 3` division polynomials `ОҰвӮғ`/`ОЁSqвӮғ` for secp256k1 вҖ” canonical + explicit forms (`secp256k1_ОҰвӮғ`: `ОҰ 3 = XВ·ОЁвӮғ вҲ’ preОЁвӮ„В·ОЁвӮӮSq`; `secp256k1_ОЁSqвӮғ`: `ОЁSq 3 = ОЁвӮғ` вҖ” Mathlib's canonical `ОҰ_three`/`ОЁSq_three` specialised to secp256k1. Substituting the concrete `ОЁвӮғ = 3XвҒҙ+84X`, `preОЁвӮ„ = 2XвҒ¶+280XВівҲ’784`, `ОЁвӮӮSq = 4XВі+28` gives the explicit degree-9 numerator `ОҰвӮғ(x) = xвҒ№вҲ’672xвҒ¶+2352xВі+21952` (`secp256k1_ОҰвӮғ_eval`) and degree-8 denominator `ОЁSqвӮғ(x) = 9xвҒё+504xвҒө+7056x` (`secp256k1_ОЁSqвӮғ_eval`) of `x(3вҖўP) = ОҰвӮғ/ОЁSqвӮғ`. Groundwork for node N7@3 and the degree half of N10(i) (`deg [n] = n`): the numerator degree `9 = 3` is the general `deg [n] = n` prediction at `n = 3`. Ring identities in `рқ”Ҫ_p[X]`, no `native_decide`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_triple_x_eq_ОҰвӮғ_div_ОЁSqвӮғ
theorems.claim: Multiplication-by-3 `x`-coordinate formula for secp256k1 вҖ” `x(3вҖўP) = ОҰвӮғ/ОЁSqвӮғ` (`secp256k1_triple_x_eq_ОҰвӮғ_div_ОЁSqвӮғ`: for `P=(x,y)` on `y=xВі+7` with `yвү 0`, `ОЁвӮғ(x)вү 0`, and the chord-tangent slopes `sвӮӮ` (`2yВ·sвӮӮ=3x`) / `sвӮғ` (secant `2PвҶ’P`, the `FiveTorsionBridge` parametrisation), the tripled `x`-coordinate `sвӮғвҲ’(sвӮӮвҲ’2x)вҲ’x = (ОҰ 3)(x)/(ОЁSq 3)(x)` in Mathlib's canonical division polynomials. Node N7@3 вҖ” extends the `n=2` case `secp256k1_double_x_eq_ОҰвӮӮ_div_ОЁвӮӮSq`, connecting the concrete `ОҰвӮғ`/`ОЁSqвӮғ` forms to the actual elliptic-curve group law. Proof mirrors `five_core`: square the secant relation to clear `sвӮғ`, then a CAS-derived `linear_combination` of the slope + curve equations after clearing `(2y)вҒҙ`; certificate `scripts/certs/triple_mult_formula_check.py` (`CERT_OK`). No `native_decide`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_torsion_iff_principal
theorems.claim: Weil-pairing foundations, rung 1 вҖ” torsion вҹә principal divisor (`secp256k1_torsion_iff_principal`: a point `P` of secp256k1 is `n`-torsion `вҹә n вҖў toClass P = 0`, i.e. iff the divisor `nВ·([P] вҲ’ [O])` is principal in the coordinate ring `F[secp256k1]`. Obtained by transporting `n`-torsion across Mathlib's AbelвҖ“Jacobi map `toClass : W.Point вҶ’+ Additive (ClassGroup W.CoordinateRing)` вҖ” the injective embedding `E вҶӘ Pic` on which Mathlib *builds* the group law вҖ” via `toClass_eq_zero` + `map_nsmul`. This is the first genuine rung of the Weil pairing construction: principality of `nВ·([P] вҲ’ [O])` is the existence precondition for the Miller function `f_P`. Key finding: the divisor-class substrate (`toClass`, `FunctionField`, `rootsOfUnity`) is already in Mathlib, so the pairing is more reachable than "from zero" вҖ” remaining rungs (extract `f_P`, evaluate at divisors, Weil reciprocity, bilinear `eвӮҷвҶ’ОјвӮҷ`) are the open frontier, `notes/FOUNDATIONS.md`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_miller_function_exists
theorems.claim: Weil-pairing foundations, rung 2 вҖ” the Miller function exists (`secp256k1_miller_function_exists`: for a nonzero `n`-torsion point `P=(x,y)` of secp256k1, there exists `f_P` in the function field `F(secp256k1)` generating the principal fractional ideal `(вҹЁXвҲ’x, YвҲ’yвҹ©)вҒҝ = (XYIdeal' h)вҒҝ` вҖ” the Miller function, whose divisor is `nВ·([P] вҲ’ [O])`. Extracted from rung 1: `n`-torsion trivializes the class `ClassGroup.mk ((XYIdeal' h)вҒҝ) = (mk (XYIdeal' h))вҒҝ = 1`, and Mathlib's `ClassGroup.mk_eq_one_iff` turns a trivial class into a principal ideal, whose generator is `f_P`. Rung W2 of the Weil sub-ladder вҖ” the input to Weil reciprocity / the pairing `eвӮҷ`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_miller_function_unique
theorems.claim: Weil-pairing foundations, rung 3 вҖ” the Miller function is unique up to a unit (`secp256k1_miller_function_unique`: two generators `f, g` of the same principal ideal `(XYIdeal' h)вҒҝ` differ by a unit of the coordinate ring, `вҲғ u : F[secp256k1]ЛЈ, u вҖў f = g` вҖ” the well-definedness input for the Weil pairing `eвӮҷ` (its value must not depend on the chosen Miller function). Representative-independence half of rung W3. Proof designed by the Fable model and kernel-verified вҖ” a piloted "strong-model + Lean-kernel" loop: Fable read the Mathlib source, identified `Submodule.span_singleton_eq_span_singleton`, and produced the proof, which the kernel accepted)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalAt`, `evalAt_surjective`, `evalAt_ker
theorems.claim: Weil-pairing infrastructure вҖ” point-evaluation homomorphism (`evalAt`: for a rational point `(x,y)` on any Weierstrass curve over a field, evaluation of a regular function at `P` as a ring hom `F[E] вҶ’+* F`, built from Mathlib's `quotientXYIdealEquiv` (`F[E]/вҹЁXвҲ’x,YвҲ’yвҹ© вүғ F`); `evalAt_surjective` (every value attained вҖ” `P` is `F`-rational) and `evalAt_ker` (kernel `= вҹЁXвҲ’x, YвҲ’yвҹ©`, the maximal ideal of `P` вҖ” the functions vanishing at `P`). The first rung of the function-evaluation layer Mathlib lacks (v4.31 has no rational-function evaluation API and `F[E]` is not known to be Dedekind there); regular-function evaluation now exists, the rational extension via localization is next. Unblocks the Weil pairing's `f_P(D_Q)` step)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.xyIdeal_isMaximal
theorems.claim: A rational point is a closed point (maximal ideal) (`xyIdeal_isMaximal`: for `(x,y)` on any Weierstrass curve over a field, the ideal `вҹЁXвҲ’x, YвҲ’yвҹ©` of `F[E]` is maximal вҖ” since the quotient `F[E]/вҹЁXвҲ’x,YвҲ’yвҹ© вүғ F` is a field. Establishes each `F`-rational point as a closed point of the affine curve; the prerequisite for localizing at `P` (the local ring `F[E]_P`) to evaluate rational functions regular at `P` вҖ” the next infrastructure rung toward the Weil pairing's `f_P(D_Q)`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalRatAt_algebraMap
theorems.claim: evalRatAt` extends `evalAt` (`evalRatAt_algebraMap`: for `(x,y)` on any Weierstrass curve over a field and a regular function `r вҲҲ F[E]`, the value of its image in the local ring `F[E]_P = Localization.AtPrime вҹЁXвҲ’x,YвҲ’yвҹ©` under `evalRatAt` equals its direct value `evalAt h r`. The correctness certificate that rational-function evaluation genuinely extends regular-function evaluation вҖ” the residue of the localization at `P` coincides *definitionally* with the quotient by `P`'s maximal ideal. Closes the evaluation layer before the Miller function `f_P` is evaluated; proof developed on the warm-server `lake env lean` loop)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.xyIdeal_ne_of_x_ne
theorems.claim: Distinct x-coordinates вҮ’ distinct closed points (`xyIdeal_ne_of_x_ne`: rational points on a Weierstrass curve with `xвӮҒ вү  xвӮӮ` give different maximal ideals `вҹЁXвҲ’x, YвҲ’yвҹ©` of `F[E]`. Proof: `XClass xвӮҒ вҲ’ XClass xвӮӮ = algebraMap F F[E] (xвӮӮвҲ’xвӮҒ)` is a unit when `xвӮҒ вү  xвӮӮ`; equal ideals would force that unit into a maximal (proper) ideal вҖ” contradiction. The divisor-support separation the Weil pairing needs вҖ” the points of `D_Q` chosen to compute `f_P(D_Q)` must avoid `P`, `O`, which requires distinct points to be genuinely distinct closed points; scouted + proved on the warm-server loop)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.xyIdeal_ne_of_ne` (+ `xyIdeal_ne_of_y_ne`)
theorems.claim: Distinct points вҮ’ distinct closed points (full separation) (`xyIdeal_ne_of_ne`: for rational points `(xвӮҒ,yвӮҒ) вү  (xвӮӮ,yвӮӮ)` on a Weierstrass curve the maximal ideals `вҹЁXвҲ’x, YвҲ’yвҹ©` differ вҖ” combining the x-coordinate case with the `YClass` companion `xyIdeal_ne_of_y_ne` (`yвӮҒ вү  yвӮӮ вҮ’` distinct ideals, same unit-in-a-maximal-ideal argument on `Y вҲ’ В·`). The complete divisor-support-separation lemma for the Weil pairing: any two distinct rational points are distinct closed points of `F[E]`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalRatAt_eq_zero_iff
theorems.claim: Weil W3 вҖ” zero locus of point-evaluation (`evalRatAt_eq_zero_iff`: a rational function regular at `P` vanishes at `P` iff it lies in the maximal ideal of the local ring `F[E]_P`. From `evalRatAt = residueFieldEquiv вҲҳ residue`: residue-field iso injective, so `evalRatAt r = 0 вҶ” residue r = 0 вҶ” r вҲҲ рқ”Ә`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalRatAt_ne_zero_iff_isUnit
theorems.claim: Weil W3 вҖ” nonvanishing criterion at a point (`evalRatAt_ne_zero_iff_isUnit`: a rational function regular at `P` is nonzero at `P` iff it is a unit of the local ring `F[E]_P` вҖ” exactly the condition to evaluate a Miller function `f_P` to a nonzero value in `рқ”Ҫ_p`, off its zero/pole support. Completes the *point-evaluation* half of Weil rung W3; `f_P(D_Q)` over a whole divisor and Weil reciprocity (W4) remain genuine Mathlib gaps вҖ” see `BARRIERS.md`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_psi3_root_iff_class_torsion
theorems.claim: ПҲвӮғ root вҹә Miller divisor `3В·([P]вҲ’[O])` principal (`secp256k1_psi3_root_iff_class_torsion`: for a nonzero affine `P=(x,y)` of secp256k1, `(ПҲ 3).evalEval x y = 0 вҶ” 3 вҖў toClass P = 0` вҖ” the 3-division polynomial vanishes at `P` iff the AbelвҖ“Jacobi class is 3-torsion, i.e. iff `3В·([P]вҲ’[O])` is principal. Welds the division-polynomial torsion tower to Weil divisor-class rung 1 at their shared middle `3вҖўP=0`; the first result letting a computable coordinate condition `ПҲ 3 = 0` *decide* the divisor-theoretic principality the Miller function needs)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_psi5_root_iff_class_torsion
theorems.claim: ПҲвӮ… root вҹә Miller divisor `5В·([P]вҲ’[O])` principal (`secp256k1_psi5_root_iff_class_torsion`: the `n=5` analogue вҖ” `(ПҲ 5).evalEval x y = 0 вҶ” 5 вҖў toClass P = 0`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_psi7_root_iff_class_torsion
theorems.claim: ПҲвӮҮ root вҹә Miller divisor `7В·([P]вҲ’[O])` principal (`secp256k1_psi7_root_iff_class_torsion`: the `n=7` analogue вҖ” `(ПҲ 7).evalEval x y = 0 вҶ” 7 вҖў toClass P = 0`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_grp_nsmul_eq_zero` (+ the `Module (ZMod n) вҶҘвҹЁGвҹ©` instance, `secp256k1GвӮҷ`)
theorems.claim: The concrete secp256k1 subgroup `вҹЁGвҹ©` is a `ZMod n`-module (`secp256k1_grp_nsmul_eq_zero`: every point of `вҹЁGвҹ© = zmultiples secp256k1G` is annihilated by `n`, so `вҹЁGвҹ©` carries a `Module (ZMod n)` structure built via `AddCommGroup.zmodModule`. This realises the abstract discrete-log model on a genuine elliptic-curve group вҖ” Mathlib provides no automatic `ZMod n`-module on a cyclic subgroup; it must be constructed from the exponent bound, which the weak keystone `addOrderOf secp256k1G = n` supplies. The bridge that lets every abstract protocol theorem instantiate on real `WeierstrassCurve.Affine.Point` values)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_dh_agree`, `secp256k1_schnorr_verify`, `secp256k1_taproot_tweak_verify`, `secp256k1_adaptor_complete
theorems.claim: Deployed signature/agreement protocols are correct on the concrete secp256k1 group (the abstract `Ecdlp.Schnorr` correctness theorems instantiated at the concrete carrier `вҶҘвҹЁGвҹ©` with base point `secp256k1GвӮҷ`, about genuine curve points: DiffieвҖ“Hellman agreement `secp256k1_dh_agree`, Schnorr/EdDSA verification `secp256k1_schnorr_verify`, Bitcoin Taproot key-tweak `secp256k1_taproot_tweak_verify` (BIP-341), adaptor-signature completeness `secp256k1_adaptor_complete` (atomic swaps / Lightning PTLCs). Contingent only on `ord(G) = n` + the abstract algebra вҖ” the honest content: the real secp256k1 protocols verify on the actual curve subgroup, not only an abstract module)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_threshold_schnorr_aggregate`, `secp256k1_musig_key_aggregate`, `secp256k1_feldman_vss_verify`, `secp256k1_threshold_elgamal_combine`, `secp256k1_schnorr_batch_verify
theorems.claim: *(instantiation, multiparty protocols)* the same concrete-`вҹЁGвҹ©` realization for the multi-signer / threshold protocols вҖ” aggregate Schnorr / Taproot multisig `secp256k1_threshold_schnorr_aggregate` (MuSig/FROST), MuSig2 key aggregation `secp256k1_musig_key_aggregate`, Feldman VSS share verification `secp256k1_feldman_vss_verify` (DKG), threshold ElGamal partial-decryption combination `secp256k1_threshold_elgamal_combine`, batch Schnorr verification `secp256k1_schnorr_batch_verify

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_grp_card`, `secp256k1_grp_isAddCyclic
theorems.claim: The secp256k1 crypto subgroup `вҹЁGвҹ©` is cyclic of order exactly `n` (`secp256k1_grp_card`: `Nat.card вҹЁGвҹ© = n`, from `Nat.card (zmultiples g) = addOrderOf g` + the weak keystone; `secp256k1_grp_isAddCyclic`: `IsAddCyclic вҹЁGвҹ©`. The group-object-level statement of "вҹЁGвҹ© is a concrete cyclic group of order `n`" вҖ” no Hasse/`#E`, only `addOrderOf G = n`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_dlogEquiv` (+ `secp256k1_dlogHom`, `_injective`, `_surjective`, `secp256k1_gen_addOrderOf`)
theorems.claim: The discrete-log map is a group isomorphism `ZMod n вүғ+ вҹЁGвҹ©` (`secp256k1_dlogEquiv`: base-point multiplication `c вҶҰ cВ·G` is an additive-group isomorphism between the scalar ring `ZMod n` and the concrete secp256k1 subgroup `вҹЁGвҹ©` вҖ” the exact group-theoretic content of the ECDLP setting: the discrete log `dlog = (c вҶҰ cВ·G)вҒ»` is a well-defined bijection. Its *computational* inversion is the open ECDLP hardness conjecture; this asserts only the unconditional bijection, via injective (`c.val < n = addOrderOf G`) + surjective (`zmultiples`) on the `ZMod n`-module `вҹЁGвҹ©`. Built with `AddEquiv.ofBijective`)

theorems.name: WeierstrassCurve.Affine.instFinitePoint
theorems.claim: Affine Weierstrass points are finite over any finite ring вҖ” general lemma (Mathlib-gap upstream candidate) (`instFinitePoint`: `Finite W.Point` for every `W : WeierstrassCurve.Affine R` with `[CommRing R] [Finite R]`, via `Point вҶӘ Option (R Г— R)`. Weakest hypotheses вҖ” no `Field`/`Nontrivial`. Mathlib v4.31.0 has no `Finite`/`Fintype` instance on any `Point` variant (gap confirmed against pinned source); natural upstream home `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/EllipticCurve/Affine/Point.lean`. Subsumes the two per-curve instances below, now one-line `inferInstance` corollaries. Pure-kernel)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.instFiniteSecp256k1Point
theorems.claim: secp256k1 rational-point group is finite (`instFiniteSecp256k1Point`: `Finite secp256k1.toAffine.Point`, now a one-line `inferInstance` corollary of the general `WeierstrassCurve.Affine.instFinitePoint` since `ZMod Secp256k1.p` is a finite commutative ring. Makes Mathlib's `Nat.card E.toAffine.Point` (as used in `EllipticCurve/LFunction.lean`) a real quantity rather than junk `0`. Pure-kernel, no native_decide)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_n_dvd_card_point
theorems.claim: n вҲЈ #E(рқ”Ҫ_p)` вҖ” base-point order divides the curve cardinality (`secp256k1_n_dvd_card_point`: `Secp256k1.n вҲЈ Nat.card secp256k1.toAffine.Point`, by Lagrange on `вҹЁGвҹ©` (order the proved `n`) + finiteness. The first verified fact about `#E` itself вҖ” all prior point-counting stayed inside `вҹЁGвҹ©`. Pins `#E вҲҲ {n, 2n, 3n, вҖҰ}`; the strong keystone `#E = n` is now proved on top, curve-specifically without Hasse/Schoof (`CurveCardinalityExact.lean`). Compiler-trusted: inherits `Lean.ofReduceBool` from the order keystone `secp256k1_generator_addOrderOf` via `secp256k1_grp_card` вҖ” the Lagrange/finiteness step itself adds no `native_decide`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_card_point_eq_n`, `secp256k1_no_nonzero_two_torsion`, `secp256k1_card_point_le
theorems.claim: The exact curve cardinality `#E(рқ”Ҫ_p) = n` вҖ” cofactor 1, without Hasse or Schoof (`secp256k1_card_point_eq_n`: `Nat.card secp256k1.toAffine.Point = Secp256k1.n`). A curve-specific certificate that upgrades `n вҲЈ #E` to equality using the special structure of `E : Y = XВі + 7` over `p вүЎ 1 (mod 3)`: `#E вүӨ 2p+1` (`secp256k1_card_point_le`, вүӨ2 `y` per `x`) and `2p+1 < 3n` force `#E вҲҲ {n, 2n}`; `2n` (even) is excluded because additive Cauchy would yield a nonzero 2-torsion point, but `E[2] = {O}` (`secp256k1_no_nonzero_two_torsion`: `2вҖўP=0 вҹ№ P=0`, since `y=0 вҹ№ xВі=вҲ’7` and `вҲ’7` is not a cube in `рқ”Ҫ_p`, `(вҲ’7)^((pвҲ’1)/3)вү 1`). Removes the last standing assumption behind Units 1вҖ“2 (the published order `n` as `#E`). Uses `native_decide` for the 256-bit non-cube leaf and the literal arithmetic (`Lean.ofReduceBool`); numeric facts independently re-checked in `scripts/certs/cardinality_secp256k1.py`, prints `CERT_OK`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_grp_eq_top`, `secp256k1_point_isAddCyclic`, `secp256k1_mem_zmultiples`, `secp256k1_glvHom_eq_zsmul_unconditional
theorems.claim: The whole point group equals `вҹЁGвҹ©` вҖ” `E(рқ”Ҫ_p)` is cyclic, `glvPoint = [О»]` unconditional (`secp256k1_grp_eq_top`: `secp256k1Grp = вҠӨ`, from `#вҹЁGвҹ© = n = #E` via `AddSubgroup.eq_top_of_card_eq`). Upgrades every `вҹЁGвҹ©`-only result to the full rational-point group now that `#E = n` is proved: `secp256k1_point_isAddCyclic` (`IsAddCyclic E(рқ”Ҫ_p)`, an instance), `secp256k1_mem_zmultiples` (every point is a multiple of `G`), and `secp256k1_glvHom_eq_zsmul_unconditional` (the GLV eigenvalue property with the `[IsAddCyclic]` hypothesis now discharged вҖ” `glvHom = [k]` with `k+k+1` annihilating every point, no remaining hypotheses). Resolves the `вҹЁGвҹ©`-vs-`E(рқ”Ҫ_p)` scope ambiguity. Pure-kernel on top of the keystone

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_pointGroupEquiv` (+ `secp256k1_point_group_equiv_exists`)
theorems.claim: The full point-group structure theorem: `E(рқ”Ҫ_p) вүғ+ в„Ө/n` (`secp256k1_pointGroupEquiv`: base-point multiplication `c вҶҰ cВ·G` is an additive-group isomorphism from the scalar ring `ZMod n` onto the entire rational-point group `E(рқ”Ҫ_p)`, not merely `вҹЁGвҹ©`. Lifts the subgroup discrete-log iso `secp256k1_dlogEquiv` off `вҹЁGвҹ©` by transporting across cofactor 1 `вҹЁGвҹ© = вҠӨ` (`AddEquiv.addSubgroupCongr secp256k1_grp_eq_top`) then stripping the `вҠӨ` subtype (`AddSubgroup.topEquiv`). The complete group-theoretic classification of secp256k1's points: cyclic of order `n`, i.e. `вү… в„Ө/n`. Unconditional given `#E = n`; computational inversion is the open ECDLP conjecture, the bijection is a theorem. Inherits `Lean.ofReduceBool` through the order keystone)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_point_group_no_proper_subgroup`, `secp256k1_nonzero_point_generates
theorems.claim: secp256k1's point group has no proper nontrivial subgroup вҖ” group-level cofactor-1 security (`secp256k1_point_group_no_proper_subgroup`: every `AddSubgroup` of `E(рқ”Ҫ_p)` is `вҠҘ` or `вҠӨ`, since `#E = n` is prime; plus `secp256k1_nonzero_point_generates`: every `P вү  0` has `zmultiples P = вҠӨ`, and an `IsSimpleAddGroup` instance). The point-group (curve) statement of small-subgroup / invalid-subgroup attack resistance вҖ” there is no small-order subgroup a point could be confined to. The geometric counterpart of the scalar-ring `secp256k1_scalar_no_proper_subgroup`, proved the same way with `secp256k1_card_point_eq_n` as the cardinality bridge. Pure-kernel on top of the keystone

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_quintuple_x_eq_ОҰвӮ…_div_ОЁSqвӮ…`, `quint_x_core
theorems.claim: N7@5 вҖ” multiplication-by-5 `x`-coordinate formula: `x(5вҖўP) = ОҰвӮ…(x)/ОЁSqвӮ…(x)` (`secp256k1_quintuple_x_eq_ОҰвӮ…_div_ОЁSqвӮ…`: whenever `5вҖўP` is an affine point `(X,Y)`, then `X = (ОҰ 5)(x)/(ОЁSq 5)(x)` вҖ” the degree-25 numerator over `preОЁвӮ…`, again with no side conditions: the generic branch adds `3P + 2P` вҖ” a chord between two nontrivial multiples, prototyping the addition step of the general N7 engine вҖ” through `quint_x_core` (12 `linear_combination` certificates incl. a 94+55-monomial master, designed by cofactor-tracked elimination of the three slopes `sвӮ…, sвӮғ, sвӮӮ`, kernel-re-verified); the 2-torsion branch (`5P = P`) and 3-torsion branch (`5P = 2P`, reduced to the `n = 2` formula) hold via `ОЁвӮӮSq вҠҘ preОЁвӮ…` / `ОЁвӮғ вҠҘ preОЁвӮ…` certificates. `ОҰ 5`/`ОЁSq 5` eval forms derived through Mathlib's `ОҰ_ofNat`/`ОЁSq_ofNat`/`preОЁ'_even` recurrences (`preОЁвӮҶ = ОЁвӮғ(preОЁвӮ… вҲ’ preОЁвӮ„)`). All certificates + 25-point (and independent 40-point, different prime) numeric checks: `scripts/certs/quint_mult_formula_check.py` (`CERT_OK`). N7 ladder now `n = 2, 3, 5`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1Bar_three_torsion_structure`, `secp256k1Bar_three_torsion_ncard`, `secp256k1Bar_torsionBy_three_card
theorems.claim: #E[3](рқ”ҪМ„_p) = 9` and `E[3] вү… (в„Ө/3)` вҖ” node N13 at `n = 3` (`secp256k1Bar_three_torsion_structure`: the 3-torsion subgroup of secp256k1 over the algebraic closure is additively `ZMod 3 Г— ZMod 3` вҖ” the full 2-dimensional `рқ”ҪвӮғ`-plane. Route: the torsion set is proved EQUAL to the explicit 9-element finset `{O} вҲӘ {(x, Вұy) : ОЁвӮғ(x) = 0}` вҖ” membership by the closure bridge, `y вү  0` at every `ОЁвӮғ`-root by `ОЁвӮӮSq вҠҘ ОЁвӮғ`, the two `y`-values distinct since `char вү  2`, exactly `4` distinct roots by the separability certificates; count `1 + 4В·2 = 9` by finset card algebra (`secp256k1Bar_three_torsion_ncard`/`_card`/`secp256k1Bar_torsionBy_three_card`); then the kernel-structure lemma N10(iii) classifies. Composes six previously landed rungs (closure bridge, exact root counts, coprimality certificate, `Вұy`-pairing, N10(iii), torsionBy vocabulary) вҖ” the endpoint `N13@3` of the `ПҲвӮҷвҶ”E[n]` critical path at `n = 3`)
theorems.method: Mathlib (finset card algebra, `torsionBy`) + closure bridge + separability + coprimality + N10(iii)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1Bar_five_torsion_structure`, `secp256k1Bar_five_nsmul_eq_zero_iff`, `secp256k1Bar_five_torsion_ncard
theorems.claim: #E[5](рқ”ҪМ„_p) = 25` and `E[5] вү… (в„Ө/5)` вҖ” N13 at `n = 5` (`secp256k1Bar_five_torsion_structure` + the closure bridge `secp256k1Bar_five_nsmul_eq_zero_iff` (`FiveTorsionBridgeBar.lean`, token-identical port of the `рқ”Ҫ_p` five-bridge incl. `five_core`): the 5-torsion set equals `{O} вҲӘ {(x, Вұy) : preОЁвӮ…(x) = 0}` вҖ” 12 distinct roots (separability certificates) Г— 2 + O = 25 = 5; `y вү  0` via `ОЁвӮӮSq вҠҘ preОЁвӮ…`; N10(iii) classifies. Replication of the E[3] pattern)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1Bar_seven_torsion_structure`, `secp256k1Bar_seven_nsmul_eq_zero_iff`, `secp256k1Bar_seven_torsion_ncard
theorems.claim: #E[7](рқ”ҪМ„_p) = 49` and `E[7] вү… (в„Ө/7)` вҖ” N13 at `n = 7` (`secp256k1Bar_seven_torsion_structure` + the closure bridge `secp256k1Bar_seven_nsmul_eq_zero_iff` (`SevenTorsionBridgeBar.lean`, token-identical port of `seven_master`/`seven_core`): 24 distinct `preОЁвӮҮ` roots Г— 2 + O = 49 = 7; `y вү  0` via the new `ОЁвӮӮSq вҠҘ preОЁвӮҮ` certificate; N10(iii) classifies. The N13 instance family is now `n вҲҲ {3, 5, 7}` вҖ” every odd prime with a landed torsion bridge; the general-`n` theorem remains the open program (separability/N10 general core))

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_quadruple_x_eq_ОҰвӮ„_div_ОЁSqвӮ„`, `quad_x_core
theorems.claim: N7@4 вҖ” multiplication-by-4 `x`-coordinate formula: `x(4вҖўP) = ОҰвӮ„(x)/ОЁSqвӮ„(x)` (`secp256k1_quadruple_x_eq_ОҰвӮ„_div_ОЁSqвӮ„`: whenever `4вҖўP` is an affine point `(X,Y)`, `X = (ОҰ 4)(x)/(ОЁSq 4)(x)` with the degree-16 monic numerator `ОҰвӮ„(x) = xвҒ¶ вҲ’ 3808xВі + 144256xвҒ° + 2985472xвҒ· + 38108672xвҒҙ + 68841472x` and degree-15 denominator `ОЁSqвӮ„ = preОЁвӮ„В·ОЁвӮӮSq` вҖ” the even rung `n = 4` of the N7 ladder (`n = 2, 3, 4, 5`). The generic branch runs doubling-of-doubling (`4P = 2В·(2P)`, two tangents вҖ” vs. the odd chords), and its crux is that the outer doubling is the `n = 2` formula applied at `X2 = x(2P)`: `quad_x_core` chains `hInner` (the `n=2` identity `(sвӮ„вҲ’2X2)(4X2Ві+28) = X2вҒҙвҲ’56X2` at `X2`), `hBridge`, `hkey`; both 2-torsion escapes (`2P = O` or `2P` 2-torsion вҹ№ `4P = O`) contradict an affine `4P`. Eval forms via Mathlib `ОҰ_ofNat`/`ОЁSq_ofNat`/`preОЁ'_even`; `ОҰвӮ„`/`ОЁSqвӮ„` independently verified against from-scratch division polynomials + 2-prime numeric `x(4P)` check (`scripts/certs/quad_mult_formula_check.py`, `CERT_OK`))

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_quadruple_y`, `quad_y_core`, `secp256k1_four_nsmul_coords_Пүfree`, `secp256k1_omega_recurrence_four`, `secp256k1_psi6_evalEval
theorems.claim: N7@4 вҖ” multiplication-by-4 `y`-coordinate formula `y(4вҖўP) = ПүвӮ„/ПҲвӮ„Ві` + fully closed `carrier_four` leaf (`secp256k1_quadruple_y`: whenever `4вҖўP` is an affine point `(X,Y)`, `YВ·ПҲвӮ„Ві = ПүвӮ„(x)` with `ПҲвӮ„ = (2xвҒ¶+280xВівҲ’784)(2y)` and the degree-24 `ПүвӮ„(x) = xвҒҙ+8624xвҲ’2875712xвҒёвҲ’16946944xвҒөвҲ’1054135040xвҲ’35487778816xвҒ№вҲ’229379784704xвҒ¶вҲ’701632282624xВівҲ’188900999168` вҖ” the `y`-companion of the landed `x(4P)=ОҰвӮ„/ОЁSqвӮ„`, and the `n=4` analogue of `y(2P)=ПүвӮӮ/(2y)Ві` / `y(3P)=ПүвӮғ/ПҲвӮғВі`. The core `quad_y_core` runs doubling-of-doubling (tangents `sвӮӮ` at `P`, `sвӮ„` at `2P`) and closes by three CAS-designed `linear_combination` certificates assembled via the exact identity `(2Y2)ВіВ·4096y = ПҲвӮ„Ві`. Fed by the freshly-derived even-index brick `secp256k1_psi6_evalEval` (`ПҲ6 = 2yВ·(3xвҒ¶+4704xВівҲ’131712xвҒ°вҲ’7639296xвҒ·вҲ’12907776xвҒҙвҲ’103262208x)`) and its anchor `secp256k1_omega_recurrence_four` (`ПҲвӮҶПҲвӮғвҲ’ПҲвӮӮПҲвӮ… = 4yВ·ПүвӮ„`), it reshapes into the joint Пү-free carrier leaf `secp256k1_four_nsmul_coords_Пүfree`, which closes `carrier_four` (both conjuncts) in the uniform induction stem вҖ” the `n=4` (even) base rung of `n7_uniform_secp256k1_x`. `ПүвӮ„`/`ПҲвӮҶ` CAS-validated (on-curve `(A,B)` representation) + 10-prime numeric `Y(4P)В·ПҲвӮ„Ві=ПүвӮ„` check. Honest scope: fixed `n=4`; the uniform induction over all `n` stays open. No `native_decide`, no new axioms)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1_cubic_separable`, `secp256k1_cubic_isCoprime_derivative`, `secp256k1_cubic_roots_card_bar`, `secp256k1_cubic_roots_nodup_bar
theorems.claim: XВі + 7` is separable вҖ” exactly 3 distinct roots over `рқ”ҪМ„_p` (`secp256k1_cubic_separable` + `secp256k1_cubic_roots_card_bar` = 3 / `_nodup_bar`: the 2-torsion cubic `XВі+7` (whose roots are the `x`-coordinates of the 2-torsion, `ОЁвӮӮSq = 4(XВі+7)`) is coprime to its derivative `3X` by a trivial BГ©zout certificate `uвӮҖВ·(XВі+7) + (vвӮҖX)В·(3X) = 1` (`uвӮҖ = 7вҒ»`, `vвӮҖ = вҲ’21вҒ»`; `p вҲӨ 21`), so it is separable and splits with `natDegree = 3` distinct roots over the closure вҖ” the `n = 2` analogue of the `secp256k1_ОЁвӮғ_separable` family, the counting brick of E[2]. Constants cert-verified `scripts/certs/cubic_separable_cert.py` (`CERT_OK`))

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Curve.secp256k1Bar_two_torsion_structure`, `secp256k1Bar_two_nsmul_eq_zero_iff`, `secp256k1Bar_two_torsion_ncard
theorems.claim: #E[2](рқ”ҪМ„_p) = 4` and `E[2] вү… (в„Ө/2)` вҖ” N13 at `n = 2`, completing the family to `n вҲҲ {2,3,5,7}` (`secp256k1Bar_two_torsion_structure` + the closure characterization `secp256k1Bar_two_nsmul_eq_zero_iff`: `2вҖўP = O вҹә y = 0`. Structurally the `n = 2` N13 instance вҖ” no multiplication formula: `2вҖўP = O вҹә P = вҲ’P вҹә y = negY = вҲ’y вҹә y = 0` (`char вү  2`), and `y = 0` on the curve forces `xВі+7 = 0`. So `E[2] = {O} вҲӘ {(x,0) : XВі+7-root}`, an explicit 4-element finset (no `Вұy` split вҖ” `y = 0` is forced), `1 + 3 = 4 = 2`; N10(iii) at prime 2 classifies. The even companion of the odd family)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.evalFracAt_well_defined`, `evalFracAt_mul`, `evalAt_unit_ne_zero`, `evalRatAt_eq_evalFracAt`, `secp256k1_miller_eval_scaling
theorems.claim: W3 evaluation layer completed at the fraction level вҖ” `evalFracAt` + the Miller-representative nonvanishing (`evalFracAt h a b := evalAt h a / evalAt h b` with the representation-independence core `evalFracAt_well_defined` (cross-multiplication `aвӮҒbвӮӮ = aвӮӮbвӮҒ` + nonvanishing denominators вҹ№ equal values вҖ” transported through the ring hom `evalAt`, no domain instance needed), Miller-loop multiplicativity `evalFracAt_mul`, unit facts (`evalAt_unit_ne_zero`: coordinate-ring units never vanish at rational points; `evalFracAt_unit_mul`), and the bridge to the localization layer `evalRatAt_eq_evalFracAt` (an `a/b` presentation of a rational function regular at `P` evaluates identically under the residue map). Plus the secp256k1 Miller-layer theorem `secp256k1_miller_eval_scaling`: the W2 Miller-function representative ambiguity is a globally nonvanishing unit factor at every rational point вҖ” the well-definedness seed for the eventual `eвӮҷ`. Honest scope: extracting an `a/b` presentation from an abstract `FunctionField` element is the documented next rung; curve-agnostic sections PR-able)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.functionField_exists_num_den`, `functionField_num_den_cross`, `evalFracAt_num_den_well_defined`, `secp256k1_miller_function_num_den
theorems.claim: Function-field `a/b` extraction вҖ” every rational function is a quotient of regular functions (`functionField_exists_num_den`: every `f` in the curve's function field (`FractionRing F[E]`, definitionally) equals `algebraMap a / algebraMap b` with `b вү  0` вҖ” via `IsLocalization.surj` on the nonZeroDivisors localization; with the division-free form (`functionField_exists_mul_num_den`), cross-multiplication faithfulness of two presentations (`functionField_num_den_cross`), nonzero-numerator (`functionField_num_ne_zero`), the weld to `evalFracAt` (`evalFracAt_num_den_well_defined`: presentations regular at `P` evaluate equally), and the Miller instantiation `secp256k1_miller_function_num_den` (the W2 generator `f_P` = an explicit quotient `вҶ‘a/вҶ‘b`, and the fractional ideal is generated by that quotient). Honest scope: the extraction controls `b вү  0` but not *where* `b` vanishes вҖ” choosing a presentation with `b(P) вү  0` for `f` regular at `P` is the documented next rung, then `f_P(D_Q)` and W4)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.RegularAt`, `evalReg`, `evalRatAt_eq_evalReg` (+ 14 supporting)
theorems.claim: W3 closed at the function-field level вҖ” `RegularAt` and evaluation of abstract elements (`FunctionFieldRegular.lean`: the `RegularAt` predicate (`вҲғ a b, evalAt b вү  0 вҲ§ f = a/b`), the unique value `evalReg` with its defining lemmas, and the two-way bridge to the localization: an element of `Localization.AtPrime (XYIdeal P)` maps to a `RegularAt` function (via `IsLocalization.surj` + `evalAt_ker` rewriting `b вҲү XYIdeal вҹә evalAt b вү  0`), and conversely a regular presentation determines a localization element (`IsLocalization.mk'`), with the payoff weld `evalRatAt = evalReg` on the overlap. With this, every abstract function-field element regular at `P` has a well-defined value at `P` вҖ” the W3 evaluation half is closed end-to-end (`evalAt вҶ’ evalRatAt вҶ’ evalFracAt вҶ’ a/b extraction вҶ’ RegularAt`); next: `f_P(D_Q)` divisor evaluation and W4 reciprocity)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.divEval_mul`, `evalReg_mul
theorems.claim: Weil ladder W3e-1 вҖ” divisor evaluation `f((Q)вҲ’(O))` is multiplicative in the function (`divEval_mul`: for `f, g` regular at both `Q` and `O`, `divEval (fВ·g) = divEval f В· divEval g`, where `divEval f := evalReg f Q / evalReg f O` is the value of `f` at the degree-0 divisor `(Q)вҲ’(O)`; via the pointwise multiplicativity `evalReg_mul` вҖ” `evalReg (fВ·g) = evalReg f В· evalReg g` at a point, from `evalReg_eq` + `evalFracAt_mul`. The homomorphism property that makes `f_P(D_Q)` a map `E вҶ’ ОјвӮҷ` in the Weil pairing; first evaluation-half rung of `notes/WEIL_LADDER.md`)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.divEval_smul_unit`, `divEval_smul_unit_eq`, `evalReg_smul_unit
theorems.claim: Weil ladder W3e-2 вҖ” how the Miller representative enters `divEval`, and conditional representative-independence (`divEval_smul_unit`: for `g = uВ·f` with `u` a unit of `F[E]` and `f, g` regular at `Q, O`, `divEval g = (u(Q)/u(O)) В· divEval f` вҖ” the entire dependence of `divEval` on the choice of Miller function is the single ratio `u(Q)/u(O)`, from the pointwise `evalReg_smul_unit` = `evalReg (uВ·f) = u(P)В·evalReg f` via `evalFracAt_unit_mul`; and `divEval_smul_unit_eq`: if additionally `u(Q) = u(O)` the ratio is `1`, so `divEval g = divEval f`. secp256k1 forms `secp256k1_divEval_miller_rep_scaling`/`_indep` apply this to the `secp256k1_miller_function_unique` ambiguity. Honest scope: unconditional independence needs `u(Q)=u(O)`, i.e. that units of `F[E]` are constants вҖ” absent from Mathlib v4.31, carried as an explicit hypothesis, not proved)

theorems.name: Ecdlp.Weil.secp256k1_miller_jointly_regular`, `divEval_one
theorems.claim: Weil ladder W3e-3 вҖ” the raw pairing value's domain (support-disjointness bridge) + the `divEval` unit law (`secp256k1_miller_jointly_regular`: a Miller function `f` for `(P,n)` has a fixed presentation `a/b`, and at any two points `A, B` where the single denominator `b` does not vanish вҖ” i.e. `A, B` off `supp(div f)` вҖ” `f` is regular at both, the joint-regularity hypothesis `divEval hA hB` consumes, from one geometric condition; and `divEval_one`/`evalReg_one`: `divEval 1 ((A)вҲ’(B)) = 1`, which with `divEval_mul` makes `divEval` a monoid hom on the functions regular at both points. Closes the reachable W3-evaluation scaffolding. Honest scope: this packages the raw value's domain + algebra; it does not assemble `eвӮҷ(P,Q)` вҖ” that needs Weil reciprocity, the frozen W4 no-go, since the base point `O вҲҲ supp(div f_P)`)
